This paper is highly relevant to the workshop's focus on real-world pitfalls and failures. It directly addresses the "Using Specific Concepts" and "Supporting Arguments" categories by explaining why models that perform well on standard benchmarks often fail in deployment (they learn spurious correlations or "shortcuts" rather than robust features). I will select this paper to cite in the related work and discussion sections, where it will help frame the mechanistic causes of deployment failures and highlight gaps in current training and evaluation paradigms.
